titleEn: AgXeed’s VCU brings existing tractors into the autonomous fold
titleZh: AgXeed推出VCU改装套件，将现有拖拉机纳入自动驾驶生态
summaryZh: AgXeed发布车辆控制单元（VCU），这是一款紧凑型改装套件，通过ISOBUS TIM协议将现有拖拉机接入其TraXwise规划与监控平台，实现高度自动化作业。系统支持预先规划路线和农具控制，适用于大田作物、蔬菜种植及大型农场，具备多品牌兼容性、GPS RTK导航和自主掉头等功能，但牵引式农具的倒车仍需手动操作。
translationZh:
AgXeed推出了其车辆控制单元（VCU），这是一款紧凑型改装套件，可将现有拖拉机纳入该公司的自动化生态系统。该单元通过ISOBUS TIM协议连接，将拖拉机的控制与AgXeed自有的规划与监控平台TraXwise相链接，使标准机器能够以极少的人工干预执行预先规划的操作。VCU现已列入《未来农业自动驾驶改装套件目录》。

据AgXeed首席商务官Arjan Mulder称，该系统的优势恰恰在于其软件层。他解释说：“AgXeed的技术优势在于TraXwise，这是随VCU提供的公司自有规划与监控平台。”

通过TraXwise，农民或管理者可以提前规划整个作业路线——精确到单个田块内不同苗床尺寸的级别。Mulder说：“我们的客户引导模式允许您预先规划一切，即使苗床尺寸不一——比如五个1.10米的苗床，然后一条3米的作业道，再五个苗床。这听起来可能是个小细节，但对蔬菜种植者来说具有实际价值。通过在一侧偏移10厘米，种植者就能一次通过完成整个苗床的作业——所有这些都可以在冬季田地测量完毕后提前规划。而替代方案——将拖拉机开进田地并手动测量一切——耗时更多且更容易出错。”VCU面向大田农场、蔬菜种植者和大型农业经营主体。

**多品牌兼容设计**

一旦安装，VCU将拖拉机控制与TraXwise连接，使标准车辆能够以高度自动化的方式执行预先规划的操作——包括转弯和农具使用。AgXeed将VCU最显著的特点定位为能够从单一地点预先规划每项操作，包括路线和农具。该系统设计为一个互联的多品牌平台，确保执行的一致性，减少操作员压力，同时通过一个界面实现整个农场的实时监控。

导航通过带RTK校正的GPS处理，车辆沿TraXwise生成的预规划引导线行驶。VCU支持自主前进和倒退操作，但牵引式农具的倒车仍需手动完成。系统还能在狭窄空间内自主完成地头转弯，匹配拖拉机标定的转弯半径；更紧凑的情况仍可手动处理。自主作业速度范围为0至20公里/小时。

**通过ISOBUS TIM控制农具**

农具通过ISOBUS TIM协议控制，拖拉机作为

**AgXeed的VCU让现有拖拉机加入自动驾驶行列** 29-06 | 自动驾驶/半自动驾驶系统 | 新闻

该单元通过ISOBUS TIM连接，将拖拉机的控制与AgXeed自有的规划与监控平台TraXwise相连。图片：AgXeed

AgXeed推出了其车辆控制单元（VCU），这是一款紧凑型改装套件，可将现有拖拉机纳入该公司的自动化生态系统。该单元通过ISOBUS TIM连接，将拖拉机的控制与AgXeed自有的规划与监控平台TraXwise相连——使标准机器能够在几乎无需人工干预的情况下执行预先规划好的作业。VCU现已加入《未来农业自动驾驶改装套件目录》。

据AgXeed首席商务官Arjan Mulder称，该系统的优势恰恰在于其软件层。他解释道：“AgXeed的技术优势在于TraXwise，这是该公司随VCU提供的自有规划与监控平台。”

通过TraXwise，农民或管理者可以提前规划整个作业路线——精确到具有不同苗床尺寸的单个地块。“我们的客户引导模式允许您预先规划一切，即使地块尺寸不同——比如五张一米一宽的苗床，然后是一条三米宽的作业道，接着又是五张苗床，”Mulder说。

“这听起来可能是个小细节，但对蔬菜种植者来说具有真正的实用价值。通过在一侧偏移十厘米，种植者可以一次作业就覆盖整张苗床——这一切都可以提前规划，甚至在冬天，一旦地块测量完毕就可以进行。而另一种方式——将拖拉机开进地里并手动测量一切——则更耗时且更容易出错。”VCU的目标用户是旱地农场、蔬菜种植者和大规模农业作业。

**多品牌设计**

一旦安装，VCU便将拖拉机的控制与TraXwise相连，使标准车辆能够以高度自动化的方式执行预先规划好的作业——包括转弯和农具使用。AgXeed将VCU最显著的特点定位为能够从一个地方预先规划所有作业，包括路线和农具。该系统旨在作为一个互联的多品牌平台运行，确保执行的一致性，减轻操作员压力，同时允许从一个界面实时监控整个农场。

导航通过带有RTK校正的GPS处理，车辆沿着TraXwise中生成的预规划引导线行驶。VCU支持自动前进和倒退操作，但对于牵引式农具，倒退操作仍需手动进行。该系统还能在狭窄空间内执行自动地头转弯，并与拖拉机校准后的转弯半径相匹配；在必要时，更紧凑的情况仍可手动处理。自动作业速度范围为0至20公里/小时。

**通过ISOBUS TIM控制农具**

农具通过ISOBUS TIM进行控制，拖拉机作为TIM服务器，VCU作为客户端。用于农具监控的TCU也可以连接到ISOBUS插头，并且农具上的标准ISOBUS功能在整个过程中仍然可用。如果农具需要VCU不支持的TIM功能，则该功能将不被使用。

在目前阶段，VCU作为一个高度自动化的系统运行，驾驶员负责监控周围环境。错误信息会显示在驾驶室内的平板电脑上，如果连接中断，拖拉机会自动停止。如果卫星信号丢失，RTK备用功能可维持定位8到10分钟。通过TraXwise可以实现远程监控，但一个操作员目前还不能同时监控多台机器——不过，任务可以同时规划和分派给多台机器。

VCU的目标用户是旱地农场、蔬菜种植者和大规模农业作业。AgXeed表示，当ISOBUS TIM标准准备好支持更高级别的自动驾驶（包括4级自动驾驶）时，以及整合当前未通过TIM协议传输的车辆数据时，进一步的集成开发正在进行中。只需拔掉电缆并将单元从其车顶安装支架上取下，即可将VCU移至另一台兼容的拖拉机上。

**易于在拖拉机之间转移**

AgXeed的主要商业活跃国家是德国、荷兰、澳大利亚、乌克兰和奥地利，并在加拿大和美国提供额外的设备支持。目前的商业状态是2026年限量发布，计划于2027年全面商业推广。

AgXeed强调的一个实际优势是可转移性：只需拔掉电缆并将单元从其车顶安装支架上取下，即可将VCU移至另一台兼容的拖拉机上。初始安装需要更多时间，因为必须定位导航天线，校准系统的转弯半径和天线位置，并且必须将电缆布线至驾驶室内的连接器。然而，一旦安装完成，VCU可以快速拆卸并重新定位——无论是转移到另一辆车上还是为了安全存放。

VCU的定价为12,500欧元（按2026年6月29日汇率计算为14,238美元），包含一年的RTK、数据和TraXwise订阅费用。此后的年度软件和服务费用为1,300欧元（1,481美元）。

**加入17,000+订阅者**

featuredReason: 该技术展示了如何通过低成本改装套件将传统农机升级为自动驾驶系统，对智慧畜牧行业中的大田作物和饲料种植环节具有直接借鉴意义，可提升作业效率并减少人力依赖。
contentFull: AgXeed’s VCU brings existing tractors into the autonomous fold 29-06 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsThe unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform. Photos: AgXeedAgXeed has introduced its Vehicle Control Unit (VCU), a compact retrofit kit that brings existing tractors into the company’s automated ecosystem. The unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform – allowing standard machines to carry out pre-planned operations with little manual input. The VCU has now been added to the Future Farming Autonomous Retrofit Kits Catalogue.According to Arjan Mulder, Chief Commercial Officer at AgXeed, the benefits of the system lie precisely in that software layer. ‘AgXeed’s technological strength lies in TraXwise, the company’s own planning and monitoring platform supplied with the VCU’, he explains.Through TraXwise, a farmer or manager can plan the entire working route in advance – down to the level of individual fields with varying bed dimensions. ‘Our customer guidance pattern allows you to pre-plan everything, even when you have varying dimensions – say five beds of one metre ten, then a three-metre tramline, then five beds again’, Mulder says.‘It might sound like a small detail, but for vegetable growers it has real practical value. By shifting ten centimetres on one side, growers can fit an entire bed in a single pass – and that can all be planned in advance, in winter already, as soon as the field has been measured. The alternative – taking the tractor into the field and measuring everything manually – takes more time and is more prone to error.’ The VCU is aimed at arable farms, vegetable growers and large-scale farming operations.Multi-brand by designOnce mounted, the VCU links the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, allowing standard vehicles to carry out pre-planned operations – including turns and implement use – in a highly automated way. AgXeed positions the VCU’s most distinctive feature as the ability to pre-plan every operation, both route and implements, from a single place. The system is designed to work as one connected, multi-brand platform that ensures consistent execution, reducing operator stress while enabling live monitoring of the whole farm from one interface.Navigation is handled via GPS with RTK correction, with the vehicle following pre-planned guidance lines generated in TraXwise. The VCU supports autonomous forward and reverse manoeuvring, although with trailed implements, reverse manoeuvres still need to be carried out manually. The system can also perform autonomous headland turns in tight spaces, matched to the tractor’s calibrated turning radius; tighter situations can still be handled manually where needed. Autonomous working speed ranges from 0 to 20 km/h.Implement control over ISOBUS TIMImplements are controlled over ISOBUS TIM, with the tractor functioning as the TIM server and the VCU as the client. A TCU for implement monitoring can also be connected to the ISOBUS plug, and standard ISOBUS features on implements remain available throughout. If an implement requires a TIM functionality not supported by the VCU, that function is simply left unused.At its current stage, the VCU functions as a highly automated system in which the driver supervises the surrounding environment. Errors are displayed on the in-cab tablet, and the tractor stops automatically in the event of a connection loss. An RTK fallback function maintains positioning for eight to 10 minutes if satellite signal is lost. Remote supervision is possible via TraXwise, though one operator cannot yet supervise multiple machines simultaneously – tasks can, however, be planned and dispatched to several machines at once.The VCU is aimed at arable farms, vegetable growers and large-scale farming operations. AgXeed says further integration is in development for when the ISOBUS TIM standard is ready for higher levels of autonomy, including Level 4 autonomy, as well as integration of vehicle data not currently transferred over the TIM protocol. The VCU can be moved to another compatible tractor simply by unplugging the cables and removing the unit from its roof-mounted bracket.Easy to transfer between tractorsAgXeed’s main commercially active countries are Germany, the Netherlands, Australia, Ukraine and Austria, with additional unit support in Canada and the USA. Commercial status is currently limited release for 2026, with full commercial rollout planned for 2027.One practical advantage highlighted by AgXeed is transferability: the VCU can be moved to another compatible tractor simply by unplugging the cables and removing the unit from its roof-mounted bracket. Initial installation requires more time, since the navigation antenna must be positioned and the system calibrated for turning radii and antenna placement, and cabling must be routed to the in-cab connector. Once installed, however, the VCU can be quickly removed and relocated – either to another vehicle or for secure storage.The VCU is priced at €12.500 (US $14,238 at the exchange rate of 29 June 2026), including one year of subscriptions covering RTK, data and TraXwise. Annual software and service costs thereafter amount to €1.300 (US $1,481).Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: AgXeed has introduced its Vehicle Control Unit (VCU), a compact retrofit kit that brings existing tractors into the company’s automated ecosystem. The unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform – allowing standard machines to carry out pre-planned operations with little manual input. The VCU has now been added to the Future Farming Autonomous Retrofit Kits Catalogue.
